channel.title: Curriculum Vitae on Dingyi Zhao
channel.description: Recent content in Curriculum Vitae on Dingyi Zhao

item.title: The Return of the King: Halo Mass Dominates Central Galaxy Quenching
item.description:
Abstract

We investigate the dominant physical drivers of quenching in central galaxies and find that halo mass is the strongest controlling factor across a broad range of stellar masses and environments.

Publication Details

  • Authors: Dingyi Zhao, Yingjie Peng, et al.
  • Venue: Nature (manuscript in preparation).
  • Preprint: arXiv:2509.01234.

Notes

  • Status: manuscript in preparation.
  • Preprint: arXiv:2509.01234.

item.title: Diffusion Model for Kinematics Generation
item.description:
What It Does

  • Learns image-to-physics mappings using diffusion models.
  • Generates velocity and mass-distribution fields.
  • Supports uncertainty-aware inference.

Why It Matters

This project extends modern generative AI to an astrophysical inverse problem with direct scientific utility.

item.title: Inferring Resolved Kinematics from Multi-Band Imaging
item.description:
This direction develops computer-vision models that map imaging data to resolved physical fields.

Focus

  • Generative modeling for kinematic reconstruction.
  • End-to-end training with simulation-informed supervision.
  • Evaluation of physical consistency and uncertainty.

Outcome

The method aims to make kinematic information accessible at scale for surveys without IFU-level spectroscopy.

item.title: One-Halo Galactic Conformity Reanalysis
item.description:
What It Does

  • Reconstructs conformity measurements with updated halo-mass estimates.
  • Applies strict controls for mass and environment.
  • Tests robustness across subsamples and model choices.

Why It Matters

A cleaner measurement clarifies whether conformity reflects halo-driven processes or sample-selection effects.

item.title: Revisiting One-Halo Galactic Conformity in the Local Universe
item.description: This project revisits one-halo conformity with improved halo mass estimation and controlled sample construction to reassess the signal strength and interpretation.

item.title: Halo Mass as a Driver of Central Galaxy Quenching
item.description:
This project investigates how halo-scale processes regulate star formation shutdown in central galaxies.

Focus

  • Compare halo mass against stellar mass and structural indicators.
  • Build controlled samples to separate confounding effects.
  • Quantify quenching trends across mass and environment bins.

Outcome

Results support the interpretation that halo mass plays a dominant role in central-galaxy quenching.

item.title: Generating Resolved Kinematics and Mass Distribution from Multi-Band Imaging
item.description: This work proposes a generative modeling approach to infer resolved kinematic and mass-distribution information directly from multi-band imaging data.

item.title: SDSS Halo Mass Pipeline
item.description:
What It Does

  • Ingests SDSS group-level observables.
  • Trains and evaluates halo-mass estimators.
  • Produces calibrated predictions and diagnostics.

Why It Matters

The pipeline provides a stable foundation for group catalog analysis and downstream studies on quenching and environment.

item.title: Machine Learning for Halo Mass Estimation
item.description:
This line of work focuses on robust halo mass estimation for large galaxy group catalogs, with an emphasis on reducing systematic bias and improving precision.

Focus

  • Feature design from group observables.
  • Model comparison across tree-based and neural methods.
  • Validation against simulations and observed scaling relations.

Outcome

The framework supports more reliable halo-based studies of quenching, environment, and mass assembly history.

item.title: From Halos to Galaxies VI: Improved Halo Mass Estimation for SDSS Groups
item.description: This paper presents an improved framework for halo mass estimation in SDSS galaxy groups and provides updated constraints on the halo mass function.

item.title: From Easy to Difficult: Inferring Halo Mass and Stellar Kinematics
item.description:
Event

Conference for Key Questions in CSST Galaxy Science Research, Guilin, China.

Talk Summary

Presented a progression from group-level properties to imaging-based inference for halo mass and stellar kinematics.

item.title: Accurate Estimation of Dark Matter Halo Mass
item.description:
Event

Graduate Research Innovation Practice Competition, School of Astronomy and Space Sciences, Nanjing University.

Talk Summary

Shared methodology and validation strategy for high-accuracy halo mass estimation in galaxy group datasets.

item.title: Galaxy-Halo Connection in Simulation
item.description:
Event

CSST AGN and Galaxy Discussion, Peking University.

Talk Summary

Reviewed simulation-based evidence for galaxy-halo coupling and discussed implications for observational inference.

item.title: Methods to Measure Dark Matter Halo Mass
item.description:
Event

CSST AGN and Galaxy Discussion, Peking University.

Talk Summary

Introduced practical approaches to halo mass measurement and compared their assumptions, strengths, and limitations.
